summary: Third-pass review finds 4 new blocking issues that did not exist in pass-2 because they emerge from pass-2's own fixes. Most critical: S-PASS3-02 — no MeterProvider is wired anywhere in src/; metrics.get_meter() returns NoOpMeter whose counters silently no-op, and the W5 swallow is designed to make broken exports invisible. Phase 8 ships telemetry that may export zero data in production, and the A9 tracer-bullet has nothing to validate against. Second-most-critical: Q9 — Decision 2 Option A (tutorial-replay = telemetry-only / non-decision read) violates CLAUDE.md audit primacy; a user-write-intent signal is not the non-decision-read superset exception, and the option should be removed from the operator menu (not left as a defaultable choice). Also blocking: S-PASS3-01 (W4 denominator mode_changed=True filter measures set-rate not transition-rate, dashboard consumers will misinterpret) and Q7 (A4 commit-trailer 'MUST' has no enforcement mechanism — convention rot vector). Five Reality warnings (state/ vs stores/, missing preferences.ts) plus four Systems warnings (operator-gate silence inverts CLAUDE.md silence rule, B1-r2 regression test is point-defense only, S4/S5 lack enforcement actors). The trajectory is now diminishing-returns territory: each fix cycle resolves named issues but the new code surface introduces a smaller, deeper set of new issues. Remaining findings are operator-grade architectural decisions, not mechanical fixes — recommend the operator make the four structural calls (split phase / wire MeterProvider / remove Option A / rewrite W4 prose) and ship rather than running a fourth fix cycle.

trajectory.interpretation: Each pass resolves its named blockers but pass-N fixes seed pass-N+1 blockers. Pass-3 blockers (no MeterProvider, Option A violates primacy, W4 denominator semantic mismatch, A4 unenforceable MUST) are all consequences of pass-2 additions — they were not latent in pass-2. The plan has reached a stable shape where remaining findings are operator-grade architectural decisions (wire MeterProvider, split phase, remove menu option, rewrite prose), not mechanical writer fixes. A fourth fix cycle is unlikely to drive blocker count to zero — more likely it converts these four into a different four. Recommendation: operator decides the four structural items now and the plan ships; further iteration is not productive.
trajectory.plan_size_drift: 3538 lines — Q13 estimates 30-40% is pass-history meta-commentary; document has become a review log alongside implementer instructions.

blocking_issues.issue: No MeterProvider wired anywhere in src/. grep for MeterProvider/set_meter_provider returns zero hits. opentelemetry.metrics.get_meter() in a process without a configured MeterProvider returns a NoOpMeter whose counters silently no-op. W5's deliberate exception swallow at the emit site is explicitly designed to make broken exports invisible. Phase 8's entire telemetry harvest (W4, W8-r2, A9 tracer counter, every Task 2/3/4 counter) may export zero data in production. A9 tracer-bullet Step 4 has nothing to validate against — the validation step itself is hollow.
blocking_issues.evidence: Plan §Task 1/2/3/4 telemetry emits; W5 wrap scope; absence of MeterProvider in src/ (Systems probe)
blocking_issues.introduced_by: pass-2 telemetry additions assumed a working pipeline; pass-1/2 reviewers did not probe upstream
blocking_issues.resolution: Operator decision. Three options: (a) Cite where the web service's MeterProvider lives and add the citation to the plan; if it lives outside src/ (e.g. bootstrap script, env), add a smoke test that proves the counters reach an exporter in staging. (b) Add a precondition task to Phase 8 that wires a MeterProvider before any counter task. (c) Explicitly defer A9 tracer Step 4 and all counter validation to Phase 9, and re-scope Phase 8 to schema/service refactors only. The current plan's Step 4 'validate the tracer counter surfaces' is non-actionable as written.

blocking_issues.issue: Decision 2 Option A (tutorial-replay counter = telemetry-only, justified as 'non-decision read') violates CLAUDE.md audit primacy. The non-decision-read superset exception covers reads with no user-write-intent (e.g. dashboard counts of cache hits). Tutorial-replay is a user gesture that signals intent and may inform later product decisions; it is exactly the class of signal that belongs in the audit trail OR is deferred until it does. Leaving Option A in the operator menu invites a default-accept that ships a policy violation. The architecture reviewer flags this as a menu-design defect: this option should not be a legitimate operator-choice.
blocking_issues.evidence: CLAUDE.md 'Telemetry and Logging' — audit primacy + non-decision-read superset rule; plan §Decision 2 §Option A
blocking_issues.introduced_by: pass-2 fix cycle (Decision 2 was added in response to A6/A8 about counter scope)
blocking_issues.resolution: Remove Option A from Decision 2's menu. Default to Option C (defer the tutorial-replay counter to Phase 9 where the audit-row design can be done alongside the user-facing replay UI). Option B (telemetry + simultaneous audit row) remains acceptable if operator wants the signal in Phase 8.

blocking_issues.issue: W4 denominator prose is semantically wrong. The mode_changed=True filter at preferences/service.py:296 fires whenever the PATCH body INCLUDED the default_mode field — not when the value actually changed from its prior. A user who PATCHes default_mode='guided' three times in a row produces three mode_changed=True events and zero real transitions. W4's ratio prose describes a transition rate but the underlying signal is a set-rate; no-op re-PATCHes inflate both numerator and denominator. Dashboard consumers reading 'mode change rate' will misinterpret.
blocking_issues.introduced_by: pass-2 fix cycle (W4 was added as a derived metric)
blocking_issues.resolution: Two acceptable fixes: (a) Rewrite W4 prose to say 'PATCH set-rate including no-op re-sets' and rename the metric to reflect that. (b) Gate the numerator to fire only when prior != current (true transition); document the data-source change in the W4 entry and update the dashboard query example. Path (b) is preferred because dashboard semantics survive future code changes; path (a) requires every future consumer to read the fine print.
missed_by: reality (noted mode_changed=True exists but didn't trace semantics)

blocking_issues.issue: A4 commit-trailer convention claimed as 'MUST' has no enforcement mechanism. No commit-msg hook checks for the trailer; no CI job greps the trailer in PR commits; no pre-merge gate fails when it is absent. CLAUDE.md institutional-memory principle: a constraint that isn't mechanically enforced (types, tests, CI, named constants) won't survive cross-session memory loss. Conventions claimed as mandatory rot fastest because the next session has no way to discover the convention exists.
blocking_issues.evidence: Plan §A4 commit-trailer; absence of commit-msg hook + CI grep job
blocking_issues.introduced_by: pass-2 fix cycle (A4 was added to mark Phase-8-introduced telemetry)
blocking_issues.resolution: Either (a) downgrade A4 from MUST to SHOULD with explicit acknowledgement that the convention is non-enforced and will drift, or (b) add a commit-msg hook + CI grep job in the same Phase 8 commit that introduces the convention. Do not ship 'MUST' without enforcement — that is exactly the pattern CLAUDE.md 'team culture doesn't protect you' is written to prevent.

warnings.issue: Plan cites src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/state/ directory; actual is src/stores/ (plural). Multiple sites including the Q9 probe command. Silent zero hits would cause the implementer to add a duplicate archiveSession action.
warnings.recommendation: Replace state/ -> stores/ across all references; re-run the Q9 probe against the corrected path.

warnings.issue: Plan cites src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/api/preferences.ts (3 occurrences) but no such file exists. updateComposerPreferences lives in api/client.ts:407. Task 6 Step 5/6 prescription falls apart silently.
warnings.recommendation: Replace api/preferences.ts -> api/client.ts with correct line citations; update Task 6 Step 5/6 to reflect actual surface.

warnings.issue: Both operator-gate defaults invert CLAUDE.md silence rule. Decision 1 defaults to Option B (status quo monolith) — the HIGHER-risk path per architecture's own analysis. Decision 2 defaults to Option A (the audit-primacy violation per Q9). An operator who simply approves Phase 8 ships the riskier path on BOTH gates with no acknowledgement that the gate was skipped.
warnings.recommendation: Flip Decision 1 default to Option A (split phase). Remove Decision 2 Option A per B2-r3 above; default the remaining menu to Option C (defer). Both decisions should require explicit operator choice — no silent-accept path.

warnings.issue: Plan is now 3538 lines, ~30-40% pass-history meta-commentary. Has become a review log, not implementer instructions. If Decision 1 picks Option A (phase split), restructure into stripped 8a/8b/8c files + archived review-history sibling.
warnings.recommendation: If phase split is approved, split the plan files at the same time. If status-quo monolith is approved, extract the pass-history into a sibling 20-phase-8.review-log.md and leave only resolution notes in the main plan.

warnings.issue: W5 try/except is applied only to Phase-8-added emit sites. Pre-Phase-8 routes that emit telemetry can still 500 from a broken exporter. Filing as a follow-up ticket recreates the 'parallel paths' pattern CLAUDE.md 'No Legacy Code Policy' forbids.
warnings.recommendation: Apply the W5 wrap to all pre-Phase-8 emit sites in the same Phase 8 commit. If too large, document the file-by-file list and the commits that close the gap, with an explicit Phase 8a/8b boundary.

warnings.issue: B1-r2 regression test is point-defense. Catches from_mode='guided' on the per-session helper — one direction of one cross-vocabulary leak. The archetype (shared Literal across distinct DB-column vocabularies) is not closed. Every new counter is a B1-r2-shaped landmine.
warnings.recommendation: Add a structural invariant: a CI check or type test that asserts no Literal name is reused across two columns whose value-set differs. Or restructure to derive Literals from the DB schema enum so the collision is mechanically impossible.

warnings.issue: S4 TTL has no enforcement actor. Same pattern as project's documented cicd-allowlist-audit_2026-05-19 memory (51% growth in 32 days because no expiry mechanism). TTLs without an enforcement actor rot.
warnings.recommendation: Either name the actor (a calendar reminder, a CI job that opens an issue at expiry) or remove the TTL claim. Do not ship a TTL that nothing enforces.

warnings.issue: S5 scope conversation has no fail-closed mechanism. An operator approving Phase 8 cold skips the scope conversation entirely.
warnings.recommendation: Make the scope conversation a checklist gate at the top of the plan with explicit yes/no answers required before execution. Or fold the four Phase 8 sub-task questions into Decision 1's menu so they are answered as part of the operator gate.

warnings.issue: Account-level default_mode PATCH lacks a Q5-shaped Pydantic boundary test. Q5 only covered tutorial_completed; the default_mode field needs the same boundary coverage.
warnings.recommendation: Add a parametrised boundary test mirroring Q5's tutorial_completed test, covering each member of the account-level _AccountModeName Literal plus a known-invalid value.

warnings.issue: Task 8 Step 8 says 'verify the new counters surface' without enumerating which. Implementer may check tracer counter and skip others.
warnings.recommendation: Enumerate the counter list inline in Step 8 (every counter Phase 8 adds, by name) and require a positive check for each before the step is marked complete.

warnings.issue: No post-deploy health probe after staging restart in Task 8.
warnings.recommendation: Add a /healthz (or equivalent) probe with explicit pass criteria after the staging restart step; fail the task if the probe does not return 200 within the timeout.

warnings.issue: composer.phase_8.probe_failed_total lacks explicit primacy justification in Telemetry primacy section.
warnings.recommendation: Add a one-line justification (probe failures are operational signals — telemetry-only because they have no user-write-intent and no audit consequence).

warnings.issue: 4 of 6 W1 pre-flight probes miss today. The operator scope conversation will likely defer most Phase 8 sub-tasks — but the plan doesn't reflect this yet.
warnings.recommendation: Run the corrected probes (W1-r3 + W2-r3 path fixes) against today's tree and update the plan's expected hit-rate. If the corrected hit-rate is still <50%, that is the empirical answer to Decision 1 — split the phase.

recommendations.suggestion: The four blockers are operator-grade architectural decisions, not mechanical fixes. Recommend the operator answer the four questions in one sitting and the plan ships rather than running a fourth fix cycle. Questions: (1) Wire a MeterProvider in src/ now, or defer all counter validation to Phase 9? [B1-r3] (2) Remove Decision 2 Option A from the menu? [B2-r3] (3) Rewrite W4 prose for set-rate, or gate the numerator for true-transition rate? [B3-r3] (4) Demote A4 trailer to SHOULD, or add a commit-msg hook in Phase 8? [B4-r3]

If Decision 1 picks Option A (split phase), Phase 8a's tracer bullet must include MeterProvider wiring as a precondition — otherwise the tracer counter validates nothing. If status quo monolith, the same precondition applies before any counter task.

Per W6-r3: add a structural check that no Literal name is reused across columns with distinct value-sets, or derive Literals from the DB enum so the collision is mechanically impossible. Without this, B1-r2 will recur on every new counter.
Per W4-r3 (Q13): extract pass-1/2/3 history into a sibling review-log file so the main plan is implementer-readable. 3538 lines of mixed plan + log is friction every implementer pays.

conflicts_resolved.issue: Should pass-3 fix cycle continue, or ship operator decisions now?
conflicts_resolved.reality_view: 5 warnings, low severity — path/file errors easily fixed
conflicts_resolved.architecture_view: 2 blockers + scope concerns; recommends phase split as the structural answer
conflicts_resolved.quality_view: 0 blockers — Phase 8 mechanically lands what pass-2 named
conflicts_resolved.systems_view: 2 blockers + diminishing-returns pattern explicitly called out
conflicts_resolved.resolution: Synthesizer sides with Systems' trajectory analysis. Pass-1->7, pass-2->3, pass-3->4 blockers does not project to zero; it projects to a stable 3-5 blocker shape where each fix cycle converts the four into a different four. Recommend operator decides the four blockers structurally and ships.

conflicts_resolved.issue: Q9 (Option A violates primacy) vs Quality (Q1-Q12 all verified landed)
conflicts_resolved.architecture_view: Option A should be removed from menu — it is a policy violation, not a legitimate choice
conflicts_resolved.quality_view: Pass-2 named issues all mechanically resolved
conflicts_resolved.resolution: Both correct — Quality is verifying pass-2 fix landing; Architecture is reviewing the pass-2 fixes themselves. The Decision 2 menu was a pass-2 addition that introduced a new policy violation. Treat as BLOCKING per Architecture; Quality's verification still holds for what it scoped.

conflicts_resolved.systems_view: Critical — potentially zeros every Phase 8 counter; A9 tracer validates nothing
conflicts_resolved.resolution: Per CLAUDE.md verification-before-completion principle: a counter that emits to a NoOpMeter is shipped-broken, not shipped. Highest priority. Score 36 (matches B1-r2 from pass-2 — both are 'audit/telemetry primacy inverted at the instrumented surface' findings).

reality.notes: Two path errors (state/->stores/, missing preferences.ts) are silent-zero-hit failures that would mislead implementers. Caught one info item that the W1 pre-flight gate is already failing on today's tree.

architecture.notes: Caught the policy violation (Q9 — Option A) and the unenforceable-MUST (Q7 — A4 trailer). Flagged Q13 plan-size drift as restructure trigger if phase split is approved.

quality.notes: All pass-2 named issues mechanically landed. Three warnings about missing test coverage (W-Q5 extension, staging counter enumeration, staging health check).

systems.notes: Caught the structural prerequisite (S-PASS3-02 — no MeterProvider) and the semantic gap (S-PASS3-01 — W4 denominator). Flagged the trajectory: remaining findings are operator-grade architectural decisions, not mechanical fixes. Two resolved-verified items confirm pass-2 fixes landed correctly where they did land.

next_steps.recommended_path: Operator answers the four blocker questions in one sitting; plan ships with those decisions embedded. Fourth fix cycle is not recommended — diminishing-returns pattern is established and the remaining work is structural choice, not writer iteration.
